title: abstract classes should avoid having public constructors
description: This rule detects public constructors in abstract classes using context-aware analysis. Abstract classes are intended as base classes and should not be instantiated directly, therefore public constructors are unnecessary and potentially confusing. The rule tracks abstract class declarations and identifies public constructors within their scope, excluding regular classes, comments, and string literals to ensure high accuracy.
recommendation: Change public constructors in abstract classes to protected or private. Use protected constructors when derived classes need to call them, or private constructors for internal initialization logic.

noncompliant: public abstract class Vehicle { public Vehicle() { } }
public abstract class Shape { public Shape(string name) { Name = name; } }
compliant: public abstract class Vehicle { protected Vehicle() { } }
public abstract class Shape { private Shape() { } }

checks.message: Public constructor found in abstract class - consider using protected or private visibility
